title: Object 10
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Object 10: "Backshrooms"}} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Comme}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Comme:wght@100..900&display=swap'); }} {{Blurbackground|file=Shroombg.jpg}} [[File:Backroomushroom.png|180x180px|center|link=]]
in life is the inevitability of death
and from death rises life once more
through caps, stalks, and spores
  ➢ '''DESCRIPTION'''
'''Backshrooms''' (known otherwise by the taxonomic name '''Aregnaceae''') comprise a diverse family of mushrooms residing exclusively in the Backrooms. Although '''Backshrooms''' are living organisms, they are categorized as an object due to first being documented as such; the title merely remaining for simplicity's sake. The family is separated into 4 genera''sing.'' genus; the taxonomic rank above "species" with a total of 12 known species. '''Backshrooms''' are distinguished from typical mushrooms by the pattern under their caps. As the spore-carrying gills extend to the tip of the cap, they join into pairs at the ends to resemble chevron arrows — alike to those found on [[Level 0|Level 0's]] wallpaper. '''Backshrooms''' are present in nearly all levels of the Backrooms in varying population levels, though environments with more readily available nutrients (i.e. containing an abundance of soil, decayed organic matter, etc.) are more likely to cultivate a greater diversity of '''Backshrooms'''. Most '''Backshrooms''' are highly symbiotic with other species or otherwise reactive with objects unique to the Backrooms.
'''ANOMALOUS MICROORGANISMS (XENOPLANKTON)'''
The family '''Aregnaceae''' stemmed from a single mushroom ancestor that noclipped from [[The Frontrooms|Earth]] to the Backrooms. Because nutrients in the Backrooms were far less readily available, requiring extremely specialized environments or traits, the family of mushrooms was pressured to undergo severe evolutionary changes to survive. Consequently, they developed more sophisticated means of nutrient storage and opened themselves to a broader diet. As their microbiomes reached a certain transitionary stage, the mushrooms had coincidentally created a viable environment for entities known as '''Xenoplankton.''' '''Xenoplankton,''' alongside all other anomalous life of the Backrooms, fall under the group AnomalobasiaClassified over domain in taxonomic classification, different from cellular life and viruses; these lifeforms are characterized by [[Entity Vessel Theory|psychodominous bonds]] which allow their properties to bypass the typical laws of baseline reality. While some anomalous entities grew into larger niches, '''Xenoplankton''' shrunk to microscopic sizes at approximately 50 micrometers in diameter. These anomalous microorganisms, though simple, can still effectively thrive in nearly all environments of the Backrooms through their rapid reproductive rate and wide range of properties. Because mutations are exceedingly prevalent in '''Xenoplankton,''' they can bypass reality in a number of ways to their advantage.


  ➢ '''DOMESTIC USAGE'''
Use cases for Backshrooms are regularly posted by wanderers on Shroomsite, embedded on this article for quick viewing: [[Object 10/Shroomsite|backnet154://shroomsite.net]]

  ➢ '''SPECIES'''
{{Collapse|num=1|initial='''> Open Backshrooms (Aregnaceae) cladogram'''|final='''v Close Backshrooms (Aregnaceae) cladogram'''|body= {{Blurimage|width=550px|align=center|file=Backshroomclades.png|caption=A cladogram of Aregnaceae's genuses; Psychomitus was the first to diverge from the lineage, then Amnirofila, then the remaining crown groups Eusymbiotiae and Protertia.}} }}
'''GENUS: PSYCHOMITUS'''
The genus ''Psychomitus'' is characterized by caps that possess chemicals specialized towards affecting the minds of animals. Members of this genus typically have thick pale stalks and grow on dead organic matter.
''PSYCHOMITUS RELICIENS'' // CINNAMON SHROOMS
'''CLASS: EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 0/3''' ---- [[File:WhiteMushrooms.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Cinnamon Shrooms''' are one of the most common species of '''Backshrooms''', growing in most of the [[:Category:The Main Nine|Main Nine]] levels. They are identified by their smooth, pale-brown caps which smell vaguely of cinnamon, along with off-white stalks that typically root from steep surfaces such as trees or earthy walls. As per their namesake, '''Cinnamon Shroom''' caps have a sweet, cinnamon-like taste, alongside providing stress-relieving effects when consumed. The microbiomes of '''Cinnamon Shrooms''' primarily assist in rapidly reproducing and spreading across various environments.
''PSYCHOMITUS INSANIAE'' // INSANITY SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 3/3''' ---- [[File:Psychomitus Insaniae.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Insanity Shrooms''' are most commonly found in [[Level 626]], possessing a dimpled magenta exterior. Typically in contrast to their environment due to their vibrant coloring, '''Insanity Shrooms''' are small yet potent carriers of hallucinogenic and mentally disorienting substances. These effects include hallucinations, a helpless sense of fear, and loss of energy. The chemicals are emitted passively through sacs filled with a purple gas along the stalk's exterior, which release upon being disturbed by organisms that would feed from them. However, the sacs often pop without disturbance, causing adjacent air to large clusters of '''Insanity Shrooms''' to be intoxicated by their psychoactive substances.
''PSYCHOMITUS AUREUS'' // MARIGOLD SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 0/3''' ---- [[File:GoldMushrooms.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Marigold Shrooms''' are one of the more reactive species in the genus ''Psychomitus'', with many resulting products being used for medicinal purposes. The cap is a bright and reflective yellow hue and becomes concave towards the center; at the tip, special chemicals are produced which repulse some species of fungivorous entities. While rare (only found in certain levels and specific conditions), '''Marigold Shrooms''' are useful in the production of many medicinal objects. Reactions can occur by placing the desired object in the "cup" of the live mushroom and lightly pricking the stalk with a sharp object. Placing [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] in the cup of a '''Marigold Shroom''' and waiting approximately eight hours will result in a thin, dark-yellow translucent fluid. Drinking this mixture can improve symptoms of various diseases and mitigate contraction upon exposure to small enough amounts of [[Sanguine Festivus]] or [[Hydrolitis Plague]]. Placing [[Object 27|Mixologist's Delight]] in the cup of a '''Marigold Shroom''' and waiting approximately two hours will result in a more vibrant shade and the emission of colorless fumes. Inhalation of the resulting fumes can assist in the repair of skin and muscle tissue, often used in treating wanderers following entity attacks.
'''GENUS: EUSYMBIOTIAE'''
Mushrooms of the genus ''Eusymbiotiae'' have more specialized microbiomes to support '''Xenoplankton''' than other species of ''Aregnaceae''. '''Backshrooms''' in this genus also possess folds at the base of their stalks that hold clumps of mycelium.
''EUSYMBIOTIAE DISTORTIA'' // GLITCH SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 2/3''' ---- [[File:Instabilityshroom.png|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Glitch Shrooms''' are a species of '''Backshrooms''' far larger than other members of the '''Aregnaceae''' family, at a maximum of 70 cm (2.2ft) in height. Caps and stalks of Glitch Shrooms can possess any color independent of each other, with rough speckles along the brim of the cap that are always roughly white. The cap is adorned with a singular marking that resembles a human eye, shifting itself around the surface to face any sentient organisms viewing it. In the case that multiple sentient beings are looking at one '''Glitch Shroom''' at once, it will appear to face only the viewer in each of their perspectives; how and why '''Glitch Shrooms''' do this is entirely unknown. '''Glitch Shrooms''' provide an unusually healthy environment for their microbiomes, directing approximately a quarter of their received nutrients to sustaining internal life. The '''Xenoplankton''' within are able to heavily [[Phenomenon 11|destabilize]] reality within a limited proximity of the mushroom itself.
''EUSYMBIOTIAE FERROS'' // IRON SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 1/3''' ---- [[File:Ironshroom.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Iron Shrooms''' are small orange-hued mushrooms that grow in dark, metallic levels ([[Level 2]], [[Level 8.1]], etc.). Their caps are speckled with small spikes that are turned rust-colored by their high iron content. '''Iron Shrooms''' tend to cluster around areas with dense saturations of metal, using specialized microbial feeding networks to "amplify" the nutrition received from feeding on metal and grime. A mostly pure chunk of iron develops in the stalks of '''Iron Shrooms''' as they age, being one of the primary sources of metal in the Backrooms. Many groups harvest and even cultivate this species for their consistent supply of iron.
''EUSYMBIOTIAE CARNIA'' // FLESH SHROOMS
'''CLASS: EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 0/3''' ---- [[File:LoveShrooms.jpeg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Flesh Shrooms''' grow in stacked "columns" along living or non-living surfaces, characterized by their fleshy color and thin leaflike shape. As per their namesake, '''Flesh Shrooms''' are genetically encoded with an unidentifiable mammal's muscle DNA, resulting in '''Flesh Shrooms''' having many properties akin to animal meat. '''Flesh Shrooms''', when properly cooked, have a taste described as a somewhat sweet blend of steak, bacon, and goat meat. Flesh Shrooms are commonly eaten by many wanderers across the Backrooms for their accessibility and taste in comparison to other available foods.
''EUSYMBIOTIAE ARGENTUM'' // SILVER SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 3/3''' ---- [[File:Silver Shroom.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Silver Shrooms''' tend to grow from living hosts such as trees or mosses and are identified by their prominent silver shade. '''Silver Shrooms''' are known to be obligately symbiotic with [[Entity 44|Silver Slimes]]; when a Silver Slime comes within range of a non-hosting '''Silver Shroom''', it is drawn in by the warmth and chemicals emitted by the mushroom's gills. '''Silver Shrooms''' redirect the slime to its system of hyphae (or "roots" of mushrooms) and absorb it into specialized chambers. When the Silver Slime detects a small organism on the surface and begins moving towards it, the host is alerted and feels for the organism in question to eject the slime onto. The prey provides both organisms with food, and both parties are benefitted as a result. Consumption or contact with '''Silver Shrooms''' can lead to traces of Silver Slime entering the body and consuming the hemoglobin inside, typically resulting in death.
''EUSYMBIOTIAE HALLUCINARI'' // AETHER SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 3/3''' ---- [[File:Aethershroom.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Aether Shrooms''' are matte-purple '''Backshrooms''' with a velvety exterior, often enveloped by a faint bright aura. '''Aether Shrooms''' use specialized '''Xenoplankton''' to warp nearby space upon being deformed, causing any object that breaks the mushroom to warp into itself. Although this trait exists to mutilate threats attempting to eat the mushroom, '''Aether Shrooms'''' "space-defining" properties can be exploited to a wanderer's own advantage. Spatial warping boundaries are projected in outward rays across the mushroom's surface and only activate if the '''Aether Shroom's''' body from the stalk upwards is deformed. Uprooting '''Aether Shrooms''' at the base of the mushroom body will not cause it to warp space, and therefore will allow one to carry '''Aether Shrooms''' without harm. Throwing an uprooted '''Aether Shroom''' at a threat (such as a hostile entity) will cause the mushroom to squash on contact, permanently mutilating the entity and allowing escape.
'''GENUS: PROTERTIA'''
The genus ''Protertia'', like ''Eusymbiotiae'', is defined by deep folds at the base of their stalks, however without clumps of mycelium held within. These folds instead hold a stringy substance that assists the mushroom in finding suitable places for the spores to root.
''PROTERTIA ADJACIS'' // WINDOW SHROOMS
'''CLASS: EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 2/3''' ---- [[File:Windowshroom.jpg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Window Shrooms''' are the only living species of the genus ''Protertia'', being thin-stalked translucent mushrooms that grow along the frames of [[Entity 2|Predatory Windows]]. Within the stalk folds of '''Window Shrooms''' are thin stringy hairs coated in space-sensing '''Xenoplankton''' that guide the spores to a suitable growing location, which are ejected when it wishes to reproduce. '''Window Shrooms''' primarily grow on the frames of Window entities, which wait for the Window to draw in prey before beginning growth. As the prey is consumed, whether it be human or other, it leaves residual traces of [[Phenomenon 35|psychic energy]] encoded with the prey's thoughts. '''Window Shrooms''' consume the energy and adapt specialized behaviors to draw in more prey of that kind (often by a produced scent or other chemical trace), which the Window recognizes and rewards with parts of future prey's corpses. Though hazardous to collect, '''Window Shrooms''' are edible and contain many essential nutrients.
'''GENUS: AMNIROFILA'''
'''Backshrooms''' in the genus ''Amnirofila'' have a specialized network of nutrient-delivering fibers in the stalk's outer ring, with these fibers visibly growing out along the cap's concave side.
''AMNIROFILA KHARTHIA'' // KHARTHIC SHROOMS
'''CLASS: NON-EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 2/3''' ---- [[File:Kharthic shroom.png|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Kharthic Shrooms''' have a dark, iridescent exterior that encapsulates the entirety of its surface body. They are relatively small and have stubby stalks, growing rarely in the open corners of most interior levels. Modified spore structures within '''Kharthic Shrooms''' carry a concentrated quantity of high-frequency [[Object 499|Kharthic Energy]] which are released when threatened — causing the typical effects of Kharthiact exposure to the mind when inhaled. Consuming '''Kharthic Shroom''' spores can swiftly lead to severe activations in the "fight or flight" response and mental disorientation. It is not advised to eat '''Kharthic Shrooms''' raw in hopes of keeping one alert; instead, this effect can be obtained less hazardously by consuming [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] mixed with ground-up '''Kharthic Shroom''' stalks.
''AMNIROFILA IGNIS'' // PYRO SHROOMS
'''CLASS: EDIBLE''' '''HAZARD: 1/3''' ---- [[File:PyroShrooms.jpeg|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Pyro Shrooms''' are tall, warm-hued '''Backshrooms''' appearing commonly throughout dark areas. Often appearing in a bright exterior red, '''Pyro Shrooms''' tend to stand out from their environment as a form of threat display. The stalk turns yellow at the base through a gradient, wherein filter-like pores cover the base structure. When agitated at these pores (naturally through chemical reception of fungivorous entities), the '''Pyro Shroom''' in question diverts nearby heat and oxygen to a pinpoint location on the perceived threat. This process often causes the creation of a controlled flame or extreme heat on the threat's body. With the proper technique, wanderers can use '''Pyro Shrooms'''' defensive measures as an efficient firestarter.
''AMNIROFILA REPLENS'' // CLONE SHROOMS
'''CLASS: DEPENDENT EDIBILITY''' '''HAZARD: 0/3''' ---- [[File:Stone Shroom.png|200x200px|right|link=]] '''Clone Shrooms''' are stout bulbous mushrooms that grow in two distinct stages throughout a life cycle. Clone Shrooms generally retain little of their own biomass; up to 20% of their body weight may be comprised of '''Xenoplankton''' at a given time. Upon the first stage, the '''Clone Shroom''' may be rooted in any substrate or material, as long as it is solid and hyphae are able to extend through it. In the first stage, '''Clone Shrooms''' bear microscopic hairs along their caps which are used to catch stray floating '''Xenoplankton''' and gain nutrients. The second stage involves internal replication of the '''Clone Shroom''''s substrate, wherein the surface it grows on will be reproduced in the hollow chambers of the spherical structure. Many theories have circulated regarding why this occurs in '''Clone Shrooms''', though it is believed to be a vestigial trait once necessary in reproduction; on standard substrate, such as soil, this would thereby replicate the nutrients in the soil, which are necessary for the '''Clone Shroom''''s stalk to grow rapidly and spread spores further. To an extent, any material meeting the preceding criteria can be cloned through a '''Clone Shroom''''s life cycle.
